prompt:
Ops tooling ticket. Our floor_ops Splunk app keeps asset-sweep checkpoints in a KV Store collection, and the nightly export job that mirrors it into the data lake is a pile of curl in cron that double-counts rows whenever someone saves during the run. Rebuild it as a proper TypeScript client under src/ (entry point src/index.ts; split the modules sensibly — client/transport, batch splitting, the checkpointed export). Node 26, built-in fetch, zero npm dependencies. test_kvstore.ts is the acceptance contract (run: node --test test_kvstore.ts).

Research comes first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the current official Splunk material — the KV store endpoint descriptions in the Splunk Enterprise REST API Reference on help.splunk.com, the limits.conf configuration reference ([kvstore] stanza), and the authentication-tokens page; Splunk's official SDK sources on GitHub (splunk org) also count as first-party for wire details. Blogs and Stack Overflow don't count. Pin down: the servicesNS data paths and the owner segment for shared collections, the exact query/sort/skip/limit/fields encodings and the order splunkd applies them, what a POST to a record path replaces (and whether any ETag/If-Match mechanism exists for collection data — the ticket that spawned this said 'ETag or key-aware updates'; if the docs only support one of those, say which and correct the ticket in your summary), batch_save semantics with _key and the documented per-batch document/size limits, and the messages error envelope. Our pinned contract is docs/contract.json with provenance in docs/official_sources.json; both are protected. If live docs disagree, report the conflict and leave fixtures and tests untouched. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final message.

What the tests import from ./src/index.ts (pinned shapes in docs/contract.json):
- new KvStoreClient({ baseUrl, token, app, owner? }) — owner defaults to nobody; bearer-token auth on every request, never in a URL.
- query(collection, { query?, sort?, skip?, limit?, fields? }) — JSON-encoded query parameter, documented sort syntax, unused options omitted entirely.
- insert(collection, doc) -> _key; update(collection, key, doc) -> _key posting the full replacement to the record path with the key URL-encoded; remove(collection, key).
- batchSave(collection, docs, { maxDocs?, maxBytes? }) -> saved keys in order: splits into ordered chunks by the documented default document limit and by JSON body bytes; a failed chunk throws BatchSaveError (savedKeys, failedAt, remaining, status, text) without attempting later chunks; an empty save sends nothing.
- exportAll(collection, { pageSize, checkpoint? }) -> { docs, checkpoint }: a stable export that never uses skip paging — think about what makes a page sequence stable while writers are active and which _key filter resumes a run — and on failure throws SplunkKvError carrying the last verifiable checkpoint.
- SplunkKvError (status, type, text, checkpoint?) decoded from the documented messages envelope; never contains the token.

Acceptance: node --test test_kvstore.ts — it runs a loopback fake splunkd; no real Splunk, no credentials. test_kvstore.ts and everything under docs/ are protected; don't touch them.

research.product: Splunk App Key Value Store (KV Store)
research.interface: storage/collections/data REST endpoints, Splunk Enterprise 9.x/10.x REST API reference

official_sources.title: KV store endpoint descriptions | Splunk Enterprise
official_sources.used_for: the servicesNS/{owner}/{app}/storage/collections/data/{collection} paths; query parameter syntax (JSON query with $gt/$gte/$lt/$lte/$ne and logical operators, sort field:1/field:-1 with comma-separated multi-field, skip, limit, fields projection) and the sort-then-skip-then-limit application order; single-record GET/POST/DELETE where POST replaces the entire record and responds {"_key":...}; insert response shape; Content-Type: application/json requirement; _key/_user system fields

official_sources.title: limits.conf | Splunk Enterprise (configuration file reference)
official_sources.used_for: the [kvstore] stanza limits pinned by the batch splitter: max_documents_per_batch_save default 1000 and max_size_per_batch_save_mb default 50, plus max_rows_per_query default 50000

official_sources.title: splunk/splunk-sdk-python splunklib/client.py KVStoreCollectionData (official Splunk SDK source)
official_sources.used_for: batch_save semantics in Splunk's own SDK: POST of a JSON array of documents to data/{collection}/batch_save with Content-Type application/json ('inserts or updates every document specified'); update() POSTs the replacement document to data/{collection}/{key}; query passes dict options through as JSON

official_sources.title: Use authentication tokens | Splunk Enterprise, Splunk Cloud Platform
official_sources.used_for: REST calls with Splunk authentication tokens use the Authorization: Bearer <token> header

research.language_rationale: The export tooling runs in our Node 26 ops runtime. Splunk's splunk-sdk-javascript is in maintenance and predates fetch/ESM, so the credible integration surface is the documented KV Store REST endpoints over the built-in fetch — the same wire contract splunk-sdk-python wraps.
